# Welcome to Build BERT From Scratch

A comprehensive, self-learning project to implement BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) from the ground up using PyTorch.

## What You'll Build

By the end of this project, you will have implemented:

- ✅ **PyTorch Fundamentals** - Tensors, autograd, nn.Module
- ✅ **Self-Attention Mechanism** - Query, Key, Value
- ✅ **Multi-Head Attention** - Parallel attention heads
- ✅ **Transformer Encoder** - Residual connections, layer normalization
- ✅ **Positional Embeddings** - Sinusoidal and learned
- ✅ **BERT Architecture** - Embeddings + Encoder + Pooler
- ✅ **Pretraining** - Masked Language Modeling (MLM) + Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
- ✅ **Finetuning** - Classification and Question Answering
- ✅ **Inference & Serving** - REST API with FastAPI
- ✅ **Production Optimization** - ONNX export, quantization

## Learning Path

| Task | Topic | Est. Time |
|------|-------|-----------|
| 1 | PyTorch Foundations | 2 hours |
| 2 | Self-Attention | 2 hours |
| 3 | Multi-Head Attention | 2 hours |
| 4 | Transformer Encoder | 2 hours |
| 5 | Positional Embeddings | 1 hour |
| 6 | BERT Embeddings & Encoder | 2 hours |
| 7 | BERT Pretraining (MLM + NSP) | 3 hours |
| 8 | BERT Finetuning - Classification | 2 hours |
| 9 | BERT Finetuning - QA | 2 hours |
| 10 | Inference & Model Serving | 2 hours |
| 11 | Production Optimization | 2 hours |
| 12 | Advanced Exercises | 4+ hours |

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- Basic understanding of neural networks
- Familiarity with linear algebra (matrix operations)
- PyTorch basics (covered in Task 1)

## How to Use This Project

1. **Start with Task 1** - Each task has a Jupyter notebook with explanations
2. **Implement the code** - Follow along and write your own implementations
3. **Complete exercises** - Test your understanding with exercises
4. **Check the reference** - API docs for the modular code

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/autokernel.git
cd autokernel

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # or venv\Scripts\activate on Windows

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Launch Jupyter
jupyter notebook
```

Then navigate to `tasks/task-01-pytorch-basics/notebook.ipynb` to begin!

## Project Structure

```
autokernel/
├── tasks/           # Learning modules (12 tasks)
│   └── task-*/      
│       ├── notebook.ipynb
│       └── overview.md
├── src/             # Modular Python code
│   └── bert/
├── exercises/       # Additional exercises
├── tests/           # Unit tests
└── _site/          # Built documentation
```

## Why Build BERT From Scratch?

1. **Deep Understanding** - Go beyond high-level APIs
2. **Interview Prep** - Master transformer internals
3. **Customization** - Modify architecture for your needs
4. **Research** - Build on top for novel contributions

Let's begin! 🚀
